([/ˈsteɪsi/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); [Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 스테이씨; [RR](/wiki/Revised_Romanization_of_Korean "Revised Romanization of Korean"): *Seuteissi*, acronym for **Star to a Young Culture**) is a South Korean [girl group](/wiki/Girl_group "Girl group") formed by [High Up Entertainment](/wiki/High_Up_Entertainment "High Up Entertainment"). The group is composed of six members: Sumin, [Sieun](/wiki/Park_Si-eun_(entertainer) "Park Si-eun (entertainer)"), Isa, Seeun, Yoon, and J. Mainly produced by [K-pop](/wiki/K-pop "K-pop") duo [Black Eyed Pilseung](/wiki/Black_Eyed_Pilseung "Black Eyed Pilseung"), they debuted on November 12, 2020, with the release of their debut single album *[Star to a Young Culture](/wiki/Star_to_a_Young_Culture "Star to a Young Culture")*. From September 2023 to March 2024 they embarked on their first world tour.

South Korean girl group

For other uses, see [StayC (disambiguation)](/wiki/StayC_(disambiguation) "StayC (disambiguation)"). Not to be confused with [Stacy](/wiki/Stacy_(disambiguation) "Stacy (disambiguation)").

**STAYC** ([/ˈsteɪsi/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); [Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 스테이씨; [RR](/wiki/Revised_Romanization_of_Korean "Revised Romanization of Korean"): *Seuteissi*, acronym for **Star to a Young Culture**) is a South Korean [girl group](/wiki/Girl_group "Girl group") formed by [High Up Entertainment](/wiki/High_Up_Entertainment "High Up Entertainment"). The group is composed of six members: Sumin, [Sieun](/wiki/Park_Si-eun_(entertainer) "Park Si-eun (entertainer)"), Isa, Seeun, Yoon, and J. Mainly produced by [K-pop](/wiki/K-pop "K-pop") duo [Black Eyed Pilseung](/wiki/Black_Eyed_Pilseung "Black Eyed Pilseung"), they debuted on November 12, 2020, with the release of their debut single album *[Star to a Young Culture](/wiki/Star_to_a_Young_Culture "Star to a Young Culture")*. From September 2023 to March 2024 they embarked on their first world tour.

## Name

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=STAYC&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Name")]

The group's name, STAYC, is an acronym for "Star to a Young Culture", and is meant to "reflect the act's aim of dominating pop culture".[[2]](#cite_note-hermantamar-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=STAYC&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

### 2016–2019: Pre-debut

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=STAYC&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2016–2019: Pre-debut")]

Sieun was well-known prior to debut as both the daughter of veteran singer Park Nam-jung, and for her roles in dramas such as *[The Good Wife](/wiki/The_Good_Wife_(South_Korean_TV_series) "The Good Wife (South Korean TV series)")*, *[Queen for Seven Days](/wiki/Queen_for_Seven_Days "Queen for Seven Days")*, and *[The Crowned Clown](/wiki/The_Crowned_Clown "The Crowned Clown")*. She won the Youth Acting Award in [2018 SBS Drama Awards](/wiki/2018_SBS_Drama_Awards "2018 SBS Drama Awards") for her role in *[Still 17](/wiki/Still_17 "Still 17")*.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Seeun was known as an actress, appearing in shows like *[The Guardians](/wiki/The_Guardians_(South_Korean_TV_series) "The Guardians (South Korean TV series)")* and *[Circle](/wiki/Circle_(TV_series) "Circle (TV series)")*.[[2]](#cite_note-hermantamar-2)

Prior to debut, STAYC was known as "High Up Girls", named after their company, High Up Entertainment.[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)

### 2020: Debut with *Star to a Young Culture*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=STAYC&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2020: Debut with Star to a Young Culture")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ab/STAYC_in_2020.png/250px-STAYC_in_2020.png)](/wiki/File:STAYC_in_2020.png)

STAYC in November 2020

On September 8, it was announced that [Black Eyed Pilseung](/wiki/Black_Eyed_Pilseung "Black Eyed Pilseung") of High Up Entertainment would debut their first girl group.[[4]](#cite_note-4) On October 8, High Up Entertainment announced that the group would be debuting in November.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The members were officially revealed in pairs via prologue films from October 12 to 14 (in order: Sieun, Isa, Seeun, J, Sumin, and Yoon).[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) On October 22, the title of their single album was announced to be *[Star to a Young Culture](/wiki/Star_to_a_Young_Culture "Star to a Young Culture")*, with the lead single "[So Bad](/wiki/So_Bad_(STAYC_song) "So Bad (STAYC song)")".[[9]](#cite_note-9) The teaser schedule was released the same day. The single album was announced to be produced by [Black Eyed Pilseung](/wiki/Black_Eyed_Pilseung "Black Eyed Pilseung"), known for previous [K-pop](/wiki/K-pop "K-pop") hits such as "[TT](/wiki/TT_(song) "TT (song)")", "[Touch My Body](/wiki/Touch_My_Body_(Sistar_song) "Touch My Body (Sistar song)")", "[Roller Coaster](/wiki/Roller_Coaster_(Chungha_song) "Roller Coaster (Chungha song)")", and "[Dumhdurum](/wiki/Dumhdurum "Dumhdurum")". The producing duo described the group's sound as "Teen Fresh", a combination of "Teen" and "Fresh", highlighting the group's "unique individual vocal colors".[[10]](#cite_note-10) Prior to the album's release, promotions were done through [1theK's](/wiki/1theK "1theK") [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") page. These included dance covers of groups such as [Blackpink](/wiki/Blackpink "Blackpink"), [BTS](/wiki/BTS "BTS"), and [Stray Kids](/wiki/Stray_Kids "Stray Kids"), which received over 1 million views,[[11]](#cite_note-11) and a vocal cover of songs by [Twice](/wiki/Twice "Twice") and [Red Velvet](/wiki/Red_Velvet_(group) "Red Velvet (group)") which received over 2 million views.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

On November 12, the music video for the album's lead single "So Bad" was released, receiving over 2.6 million views in the first 24 hours.[[13]](#cite_note-:0-13) Their album was released on the same day, selling over 4,300 copies on its first day, the most for a debut girl group in 2020.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] It went on to sell more than 10,000 copies on its first week, becoming the first debut album by a girl group in 2020 to do so.[[2]](#cite_note-hermantamar-2) They promoted the album through a [V Live](/wiki/V_Live "V Live") debut showcase, promoting both the lead single, "So Bad" and [B-side](/wiki/B-side "B-side") "Like This".[[14]](#cite_note-14) The group made their music show debut on November 13, 2020, at *[Music Bank](/wiki/Music_Bank_(TV_program) "Music Bank (TV program)")*,[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] which was followed up with performances at *[Show Champion](/wiki/Show_Champion "Show Champion")*, *[Inkigayo](/wiki/Inkigayo "Inkigayo")*, and *[The Show](/wiki/The_Show_(South_Korean_TV_program) "The Show (South Korean TV program)")*. The album debuted at number 17 on the weekly [Gaon Album Chart](/wiki/Gaon_Album_Chart "Gaon Album Chart"). "So Bad" debuted at number 90 on *Billboard*'s [K-pop Hot 100](/wiki/K-pop_Hot_100 "K-pop Hot 100"), and number 21 on the [World Digital Song Sales](/wiki/World_Digital_Song_Sales "World Digital Song Sales") chart.[[15]](#cite_note-world_songs_chart_forbes-15)

### 2021–2022: *Staydom*, *Stereotype*, *Young-Luv.com,* and *We Need Love*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=STAYC&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2021–2022: Staydom, Stereotype, Young-Luv.com, and We Need Love")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/97/StayCFansign.jpg/250px-StayCFansign.jpg)](/wiki/File:StayCFansign.jpg)

STAYC in May 2021

On April 1, 2021, STAYC were featured in the [YouTube Originals](/wiki/List_of_YouTube_Premium_original_programming "List of YouTube Premium original programming") docuseries *K-Pop Evolution* documenting "their trainee days" and debut process".[[16]](#cite_note-16) Eight days later, they released their second single album *[Staydom](/wiki/Staydom "Staydom")* and its lead single "[ASAP](/wiki/ASAP_(STAYC_song) "ASAP (STAYC song)")".[[17]](#cite_note-17) The music video of "ASAP" was released simultaneously with the album and reached 20 million views in nine days.[[18]](#cite_note-18) The single also entered the *Billboard* K-pop 100 weekly chart.[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20) *Staydom* sold 56,198 copies in its first month.

On September 6, the group released their first extended play *[Stereotype](/wiki/Stereotype_(EP) "Stereotype (EP)")* and its [lead single of the same name](/wiki/Stereotype_(STAYC_song) "Stereotype (STAYC song)").[[21]](#cite_note-21) The music video reached 30 million views in 21 days.[[22]](#cite_note-22) The album recorded sales of over 114,000 copies in its first week.[[23]](#cite_note-23) On September 14, the group received their first music show trophy on [SBS MTV](/wiki/SBS_MTV "SBS MTV")'s *[The Show](/wiki/The_Show_(South_Korean_TV_series) "The Show (South Korean TV series)")* with an overall score of 8,760 points.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

On February 21, 2022, STAYC released their second extended play *[Young-Luv.com](/wiki/Young-Luv.com "Young-Luv.com")* and its lead single "[Run2U](/wiki/Run2U "Run2U")".[[25]](#cite_note-25) The album debuted atop the [Gaon Album Chart](/wiki/Gaon_Album_Chart "Gaon Album Chart"), becoming their first number one album on the chart.[[26]](#cite_note-26) The single peaked at number 4 on the Gaon Digital Chart.

On July 19, STAYC released their third single album *[We Need Love](/wiki/We_Need_Love_(single_album) "We Need Love (single album)")* and its lead single "[Beautiful Monster](/wiki/Beautiful_Monster_(STAYC_song) "Beautiful Monster (STAYC song)")".[[27]](#cite_note-27) It has been announced that the group will hold their first fan meeting on August 13.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

On November 23, STAYC made their Japanese debut with the single "Poppy".[[1]](#cite_note-UMJ-1)[[29]](#cite_note-29) The single peaked at number 7 on the Oricon Singles Chart.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

### 2023–2024: *Teddy Bear*, *Teenfresh*, *Metamorphic*, and *...l*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=STAYC&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2023–2024: Teddy Bear, Teenfresh, Metamorphic, and ...l")]

On January 18, 2023, High Up Entertainment announced that STAYC would be releasing an album in February.[[31]](#cite_note-31) On February 14, STAYC released their fourth single album titled *[Teddy Bear](/wiki/Teddy_Bear_(single_album) "Teddy Bear (single album)")* alongside the [lead single of the same name](/wiki/Teddy_Bear_(STAYC_song) "Teddy Bear (STAYC song)").[[32]](#cite_note-32) The album debuted at number one on South Korea's [Circle Album Chart](/wiki/Circle_Album_Chart "Circle Album Chart") with 269,367 units sold.[[33]](#cite_note-33) The single peaked at number four on [Circle Digital Chart](/wiki/Circle_Digital_Chart "Circle Digital Chart") and number five on *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* [South Korea Songs](/wiki/South_Korea_Songs "South Korea Songs").[[34]](#cite_note-34)[[35]](#cite_note-35)

On July 23, it was announced that STAYC would be releasing their third extended play titled *[Teenfresh](/wiki/Teenfresh "Teenfresh")* on August 16.[[36]](#cite_note-36) On July 27, it was officially announced that STAYC would be embarking on their [first world tour](/wiki/Teenfresh_World_Tour "Teenfresh World Tour"), also titled Teenfresh.[[37]](#cite_note-37) They finished the tour successfully on March 10, 2024, and immediately started working on a new album.[[38]](#cite_note-38)

On June 3, 2024, High Up Entertainment announced that STAYC's first studio album *[Metamorphic](/wiki/Metamorphic_(album) "Metamorphic (album)")* would be released on July 1, with the lead single titled "[Cheeky Icy Thang](/wiki/Cheeky_Icy_Thang "Cheeky Icy Thang")".[[39]](#cite_note-39)[[40]](#cite_note-40)

On October 17, High Up Entertainment announced that STAYC would be releasing their fifth single album titled *[...l](/wiki/...l "...l")* on October 30, with the lead single "GPT".[[41]](#cite_note-41)[[42]](#cite_note-42) The Japanese version of GPT was released on November 13, along with "Tell Me Now".[[43]](#cite_note-43)

### 2025–present: *S* and *I Want It*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=STAYC&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2025–present: S and I Want It")]

On February 10, 2025, it was announced that STAYC would be kicking off their second world tour, Stay Tuned, in Seoul on April 12 and 13, while a new album was confirmed to be released in March.[[44]](#cite_note-44) On February 24, High Up Entertainment announced that the group's sixth single album *S* would be released on March 18.[[45]](#cite_note-45) On July 3, High Up Entertainment announced that STAYC would be releasing a special single album *I Want It* on July 23.[[46]](#cite_note-46)

On February 11, 2026, STAYC released their first Japanese studio album, *Stay Alive*, with the lead single "Mwuah".[[47]](#cite_note-47)
